title: Brassaiopsis resecta
taxonomy: Brassaiopsis resecta is a species of plant in the family Araliaceae. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the genus Brassaiopsis. The genus Brassaiopsis is a group of plants commonly known as the 'ginseng trees' or 'ivory palms'. These plants are native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Brassaiopsis resecta is specifically found in Southeast Asia.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular conditions.
